summary: Frontend regression for DialBox iteration 10: Dial-In Trivia (key 2), Madame Ruby guided tarot session, Zartan daytime normal reading. All 3 features PASS. No regressions on scroll, layout, or audio channel error.

test_results_detail.menu_key2_is_trivia_no_chat_bae: PASS — Live IVR Menu Preview shows '2 Dial-In Trivia'; Config Panel shows Dial-In Trivia (all ages); string 'Chat Bae' not present anywhere in menu or config.
test_results_detail.trivia_intro_and_r1q1_render: PASS — Dialing 2 shows 'Connecting to Dial-In Trivia...', intro line 'Four rounds, six questions each. Answer with the keypad, or say repeat, explain, or skip. Good luck!', then '── Round 1 of 4 · Question 1 of 6 ──', a question, 4 numbered choices, and prompt 'Press a number to answer · say "repeat", "explain" or "skip" · 0 to quit.'
test_results_detail.trivia_answer_feedback_and_advancement: PASS — Pressing 1 (Mars) triggered '✅ Correct! ... (Score: 1)'. Next question '── Round 1 of 4 · Question 2 of 6 ──' auto-rendered. Pressing 2 for Q2 (wrong) gave '❌ Not quite. The answer was 1. Hedwig. ... (Score: 1)'. Score tracked correctly; both correct and wrong feedback formats present.
test_results_detail.trivia_quit_via_0: PASS — In a prior run, pressing 0 during a question returned the caller to the DialBox main menu (menu text 'Press a number to begin. Dial ✱ for voicemail, 0 to repeat.' shown after quit).
test_results_detail.madame_ruby_guided_tarot: PASS — Dial 1 shows oracle list with '3 Madame Ruby — Velvet-voiced reader of cards and desires'. Selecting 3 prompts for name (ruby-name-input appeared). Sent 'Alex' → asked for situation (ruby-situation-input appeared). Sent 'my career' → asked style: 'Press 1 for a PREDICTION ... or 2 for REFLECTION'. Pressed 1 → after a few seconds, reading rendered with '🂠 Cards drawn: The Tower · The Empress · Queen of Cups' (three tarot cards) and a personalized reading tied to career/rebuilding, ending with 'Press ✱ to hear it again · # for another oracle · 0 for the main menu · or hang up.'
test_results_detail.zartan_daytime_normal: PASS — Dial 1 → 1 (Zartan Speaks) at daytime → 'Zartan Speaks: I'm dealing a card… The Velvet Key: ...' theatrical fortune-card reading; no explicit naughty/cheeky adult content. Night-mode gating not tested via UI (time-based; already curl-verified per review).
test_results_detail.regression_layout_controls_above_crt: PASS — Keypad button '5' y=427, CRT top y=784.5. Lift/keypad/tap-to-talk remain above the Line Monitor.
test_results_detail.regression_no_scroll_on_keypad: PASS — window.scrollY stayed at 0 across all keypad presses in every flow tested (dial 1, dial 2, digit answers, dial 0).
test_results_detail.regression_no_audio_channel_unavailable: PASS — '// audio channel unavailable' string never appeared in the Line Monitor across all flows.

observations: Ruby reading addresses the caller as 'Seeker' rather than literally saying 'Alex' — reading is tied to their situation ('career') but does not spell out the name. Per review guidance to verify by STRUCTURE, this is acceptable (AI non-deterministic).

Trivia Q -> next-Q generation can take longer than expected during 'busy' generation; observed one instance where Q3 did not render within 45s. Q1→Q2 advancement is robust; this may be worth monitoring for slow LLM latency.
During AI generation (mode 'busy'), the Line Monitor status shows 'CONNECTING' — good UX cue.
critical_code_review_comments: PhoneSimulator.jsx continues to grow (now includes Ruby + Trivia handlers on top of existing lines) — strongly recommend splitting per-line handlers into custom hooks (e.g., useRubySession, useTrivia) for maintainability. This is a persisting comment.
Trivia next-question generation latency (>10s) could benefit from client-side prefetch of the next question after answer submission, to smooth the 'busy' gap.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All three iteration-10 features (Dial-In Trivia key 2, Madame Ruby guided tarot, Zartan daytime normal) verified via live UI on REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Testids used: lift-handset-btn, ruby-name-input, ruby-situation-input, mindline-send-btn, crt-console; keypad via button:has-text('<digit>').first. Wait pattern: 2.5s after lift, 5-30s for AI-generated content (trivia Q gen, Ruby reading), poll [data-testid='crt-console'] innerText for target strings. Night-mode Zartan (naughty) is time-gated (hour>=22 or <5) and cannot be forced from UI on daytime test — main agent has curl-verified it separately.
